title: Extend stand-alone capacity manager connected board limit
excerpt:
Description
A stand-alone capacity manager object can only have 50 connected boards while portfolio component capacity managers have a 200 connected board limit.  This seems backwards, it seems like the natural use case …

posts.author: DavidDvorak (David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

A stand-alone capacity manager object can only have 50 connected boards while portfolio component capacity managers have a 200 connected board limit. This seems backwards, it seems like the natural use case for a stand-alone capacity manager is to connect resource planners across multiple portfolios (otherwise you would just use a portfolio component capacity manager). I use portfolio objects to represent each of my teams in my IT organization, I need a single capacity manager to roll up resource allocation across all teams and have one view of resource allocation

What are you trying to achieve

I need a single view of resource allocation across all the portfolios in my organization. I need to be able to provide this view without constantly managing the connected boards to my stand-alone capacity manager object.

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @DavidDvorak ,

You can try the Mirror Item Multiple Boards app.

What You Can Do -

Add the SAME ITEM ON MULTIPLE BOARDS with live sync and full context

Sync UPDATES + REPLIES in real time for items and subitems

Set up automations like “When status changes to something, mirror item to target board and group”

Create a Master Board with items/subitems from multiple boards

Add a subitem from one board as a main item on another board

Keep your boards clean and connected — no Mirror Columns or Connect Boards needed

posts.author: DavidDvorak (David)
posts.cookedHtml: No thank you…you misunderstand what I’m trying to achieve.

posts.author: Ramarm (Ram Perel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @DavidDvorak ,

I’m Ram the CTO of spot-nik. We are actually working this days on a resource planning app that I believe will fit exactly your need!

I would like to demo it for you and get feedback, and open an alpha version for you!

Please reach out at ram.perel@spot-nik.com so we can schedule a meeting and I will show you the app.

Ram

posts.author: WirkKarl (Karl)
posts.cookedHtml: It makes sense to extend the limit for stand-alone capacity managers. A single view across multiple portfolios is exactly why someone would use it, and 50 boards feels restrictive. Increasing the limit closer to what portfolio component managers have (200) would make resource planning much more practical and reduce manual management.
